title: Rockstar reponds as GTA 6 fans want disc in physical copies
body: After all the complaints about Rockstar Games seemingly scrapping discs for Grand Theft Auto 6, new reports claim that physical media lovers don’t have to worry. On June 25, Rockstar Games finally opened up pre-orders for Grand Theft Auto 6, putting an end to the rumors about the game’s price tag, as well as revealing what editions will be on offer. GTA 6 will cost fans $79.99 for the base version, and $99.99 for the ultimate edition, with the latter coming packed with pre-order bonuses including exclusive stores and a mission for the single-player side of things. Despite all the hype, some fans’ joy was dashed with claims that Rockstar wouldn’t have a physical disc for GTA 6. Pre-orders for physical copies stated that players would receive a code that would let them download the game, rather than putting a disc in their console. GTA 6 discs may be coming after all However, there have been claims that this will change come December, according to Polish outlet ppe.pl and their source, Graczdari. On June 25, they reported that fans who pre-order the physical version before November 19 will receive a code for a digital download, but beyond that, discs will be available in a second run in December. “The code will be a one-off run, followed by the option of a disc release,” they reported, according to Graczdari’s information. Rockstar Support responds to “physical copy” questions The supposed insider has been correct with information in the past; however, they have been banned from the GTA Forums over incorrect information. Graczdari previously claimed that pre-orders for the game were going to start in May. That coincided with the Best Buy leaks over their affiliate program, but nothing ultimately came from that. Related Additionally, fans have been receiving responses from Rockstar Support over their concerns about no discs, with them saying that players will be able to “obtain a physical copy in the following months.” Many fans have speculated that this is being done to prevent leaks for GTA 6, seeing as copies of GTA 5 and Red Dead Redemption 2 both leaked due to early distribution. However, the word disc still hasn’t been mentioned by Rockstar. It remains to be seen if this will be the case, and if you will have to buy a second copy of the game to receive a disc.

title: GTA 6 will use new loadout system instead of carrying unlimited weapons
body: GTA 6 looks set to limit the number of guns players can carry at any given time, marking a drastic change from previous games in the series. After months of waiting, pre-orders for GTA 6 are finally live, revealing the price and editions. The ultimate edition costs $100 and features plenty of exclusive content, including the usual bonus items and even entire stores. This upgraded version has also helped to reveal some crucial information about the game, such as the inclusion of story chapters like Red Dead Redemption 2. Well, it seems that GTA 6 is taking more cues from its western counterpart, as it appears to limit how many guns you can carry at once. GTA 6 looks set to limit number of guns you can carry at once In past GTA games, players have had infinitely deep pockets, capable of carrying an unlimited number of guns and grenades at once. We’ve become used to being one-man armies who can switch from an Assault Rifle to a Shotgun to an RPG at will. But one of the bonuses in the ultimate edition is a garage with a “weapons locker” that lets you “customize your loadout for any occasion.” This suggests Jason and Lucia won’t be able to carry everything on them at once, and we’ll have to decide which weapons to bring along. It sounds similar to the way it worked in Red Dead Redemption 2, where Arthur could only carry a couple of handguns and then two large weapons, with the rest being stored on his horse. Every time he dismounted, players could select the best guns for the task at hand. Related This was mainly to make RDR2 feel slower and more realistic, but it would be a major change for a GTA game, which is usually more about fast-paced carnage. It remains to be seen how strict these limitations are compared to Red Dead, as there might be ways to carry more guns at once, either by default or unlocked later in the story. Rockstar has promised that marketing is set to ramp up this summer, so we shouldn’t have to wait long for a more in-depth breakdown.

title: Holy Crap, Vice City Looks Amazing In This New GTA 6 Clip
body: I expected Grand Theft Auto 6 to be a big game. And every screenshot and trailer we’ve seen of it has only reinforced that idea. And yet, when I scrolled the newly updated official GTA 6 website today, I was stunned by the sheer scope of the world of Vice City. And I wasn’t alone. On June 18, Rockstar Games revealed the official cover art for GTA 6 (don’t worry, it has a helicopter on it) and also announced that pre-orders for the highly anticipated game would go live on June 25 on console storefronts and at “select retailers.” It also quietly updated the official Grand Theft Auto 6 website a bit, adding the game’s cover artwork. And more excitingly, Rockstar added a new eight-second clip of Vice City to the site as you scroll down. Thankfully, if you dig into the site’s code, you can pluck out the full, high-res video to gawk at. This is what longtime GTA leaker and dataminer TexFunz2 did, as you can see below: A new look at Vice Cityhttps://t.co/6RyGImp23Q#GTAVI pic.twitter.com/4WxuckIHUF — Tex2 (@TexFunz2) June 18, 2026 And just…wow. I have a full-resolution version of the video on my PC, and I can’t stop looking at it. The city looks incredibly detailed and dense. You can look far off into the distance and see lights and buildings that look miles and miles away. It’s truly impressive. And keep in mind that Rockstar Games has historically used in-engine footage and real gameplay for all of their trailers. So this isn’t some pre-rendered nonsense, but instead a genuine look at what Vice City will look like in GTA 6. Wowza. Reactions online were mostly positive, with some trolls claiming it looked bad, which is just sort of a wild thing to post online, but okay. “The attention to detail in this city is insane,” posted one user about the new clip. “What’s so beautiful about this is how the lights look at night time especially on the street. I just came back from Miami and the accuracy is literally insane,” said another person. Another user shared a comparison of Vice City at night as it looked in the PS2 game compared to GTA 6‘s version of the city. It really sums up how far things have come in the 20+ years since Rockstar developed that game. — BigBossSnakeWolfCorn (@BBSnakeCorn) June 18, 2026 Grand Theft Auto 6 launches on November 19 on PS5 and Xbox Series X/S. You can pre-order the game starting on June 25. I don’t expect any delays this time.

title: GTA 6′s Cover Continues The Weird Rule Rockstar Has Followed For 25 Years
body: Rockstar Games has officially revealed the cover for Grand Theft Auto 6, and the very first thing I did when I saw it was check to see if it had a helicopter in the top left corner of the artwork. And yup, it does. It’s nice to see Rockstar continuing this tradition that it has (mostly) stuck to for 25 years. On June 18, Rockstar announced that GTA 6 pre-orders would go live across console storefronts and retailers on June 25. The company didn’t release a new trailer alongside this announcement, but it did reveal the game’s official cover, which you can see below: And right there in the top left is a helicopter, as I hoped. Rockstar is continuing a little Easter egg or design quirk it has maintained on almost every GTA cover since 2001. As I’ve documented before, since the release of Grand Theft Auto III on the PS2, Rockstar has been including a helicopter of some kind in the top left portion of all of its GTA covers, with a few weird exceptions. Technically, the international versions of GTA 3‘s cover didn’t include a helicopter in that spot, but that cover also didn’t use the now-iconic grid layout with various images. So who cares about that ugly cover! GTA Advance on the GBA also didn’t include the heli, but its cover was just the GTA logo on a boring grey background, so once again, who cares about that ugly cover! There is one grid-based GTA game cover that doesn’t include a helicopter in the top left corner. Can you guess it? Well, guess fast because the answer is about to be revealed at the start of the next sentence that follows this one. Grand Theft Auto: Chinatown Wars. That’s the one time Rockstar didn’t include the heli. Some might say it’s because you couldn’t pilot helicopters in GTA: Chinatown Wars, which launched on the Nintendo DS and PSP back in 2009, but that’s not a good explanation. You couldn’t pilot helis in Grand Theft Auto 3, but it included one on the cover. Maybe Rockstar forgot? Or maybe they just wanted to see what a GTA cover would look like without a heli. I hate it. I’m so happy that the helicopter is back for GTA 6‘s cover art. It’s like a little nod to the past and a comforting constant in these wild times. No matter what, I can always count on the heli in the top left of a new GTA game. And maybe a motorcycle in the top right corner, though that’s a more obscure, lesser-known pattern that Rockstar follows less frequently. Feel free to break out that info and impress…someone. Possibly.

title: Here’s How I Know We Are Entering Rockstar’s End Game For Launching GTA 6
body: Grand Theft Auto 6 is launching on November 19. Some of you might roll your eyes at the seeming certainty of that statement, and understandably so. Rockstar and Take-Two have delayed the game a few times now. But, this time, I’m feeling very confident that GTA 6 is actually, for real, seriously launching as planned. Why? Because Rockstar has cmade all their social media accounts pink. On Thursday, Rockstar Games announced that pre-orders for GTA 6 would go live on June 25. Alongside that news came the reveal of the game’s official cover art. It looks rad and has some serious GTA Vice City on PS2 vibes. And as all of this was happening, Rockstar Games went around and changed all of the profile pics and banners found on the company’s various social media accounts. Before, they featured the old classic yellow Rockstar logo. Now, however, they all feature purple and pink palm trees with a neon-white Rockstar logo plastered on top. Why does this matter? Well, it signals to me that the company has officially entered “GTA 6 Marketing Mode.” Historically (and there’s not that much to go on because Rockstar’s slow release schedule means they haven’t put out all that many new games in the social media era), once the company switches its icons and enters this phase of marketing for a game, it doesn’t announce a new delay. You can look back at Rockstar’s Facebook page to see that once the company swapped its icon for a Red Dead Redemption 2-themed logo in May 2018, a few months after announcing a delay, the game stuck to its October 2018 launch date. Essentially, once Rockstar and Take-Two start showing off the cover, taking people’s money, and swapping social media icons and banners, you can be pretty damn sure that everyone involved is confident that the game is coming out as planned, barring some catastrophic event. So as long as the world doesn’t end between now and November, GTA 6 will launch as planned on November 19 on PS5 and Xbox.

title: GTA 6 Pre-Orders Start Next Week As Rockstar Reveals The Game’s Official Cover Art
body: Rockstar Games has announced that starting next week, you’ll be able to pre-order Grand Theft Auto 6. And while it didn’t release a new trailer alongside this news, it did reveal the official cover for the game. On June 18, Rockstar Games confirmed that you’ll be able to pre-order GTA 6 on digital storefronts and at “select retailers” starting on June 25. Yes, finally, GTA fans will be able to actually give Rockstar money for the highly anticipated next entry in the Grand Theft Auto series. I imagine this makes a lot of GTA fans and Take-Two execs very happy. No other info was revealed about whether there will be different editions. And more importantly, we still don’t know how much GTA 6 will cost. But we’ll find out the price next week. Pre-orders for Grand Theft Auto VI will officially begin on June 25 on digital storefronts and at other select retailers. Check out the official cover art, also available as downloadable artwork at https://t.co/XPwC8URCQ4 pic.twitter.com/pRVXk4eyDQ — Rockstar Games (@RockstarGames) June 18, 2026 Besides the news that GTA 6 pre-orders start soon, Rockstar also revealed the official cover art for Grand Theft Auto 6. Here’s a nice high-res image of it for you to look over cloesly: Ohhh, very pretty and colorful. I especially like the alligator hanging out on the cover. Good for him. This cover has some big GTA: Vice City on PS2 vibes, and that ain’t a bad thing in my book at all. Mostly, I’m just happy that all of the game media will have a new image to use when writing about GTA 6 between now and the game’s release in November. Hopefully, none of those stories involves a delay. If Rockstar and Take-Two are taking pre-order dollars and showing off the cover, it feels like we are actually getting this game in 2026. Wowza. Exciting stuff. And if it does get delayed out of November, well, that’s going to be really awkward for the rest of the industry that all ran away and hid in October and September. Grand Theft Auto 6 is launching on November 19 on PS5 and Xbox. Pre-orders start on June 25.

title: Thousands Search For GTA 6 Cheats Before The Game Even Exists
body: Grand Theft Auto VI does not come out until November 19. Nobody outside Rockstar Games has played it. We have not even seen real gameplay. Yet, thousands of people are searching for GTA 6 cheat codes right now, today, for a game that does not exist in anyone's hands. They are not confused. They are planning. If anything, cheats are a very Grand Theft Auto thing to do. The moment a GTA game is announced, some portion of the fanbase immediately starts thinking about how to break it. So let us get ahead of the wave and lay out what we actually know, what we can reasonably predict, and what nobody can tell you yet without lying to you. Every mainline GTA since Grand Theft Auto III has shipped with cheat codes in single-player. Grand Theft Auto V has around thirty of them, split across button combinations and in-game phone numbers. Sure, some modern games have quietly dropped these traditional mechanics, replaced them with accessibility toggles, or, worse, with paid assist features, and we're not saying Rockstar is above this. However, not even Rockstar is probably brazen enough to change the very thing that makes GTA, well, a GTA title. Spawning a Buzzard on a whim and flattening half of Los Santos is as much a part of the GTA experience as the story missions. Besides, single-player cheats cost Rockstar nothing. They don't interfere with Grand Theft Auto Online, and Rockstar already has enough real controversies on its plate. Also, in case you didn't know, cheats have never worked in GTA Online, and they never will. Anyone promising you working online cheats is either selling you a mod menu that will get your account banned, or straight-up scamming you. Most Likely GTA 6 Cheats Cheats that appeared in GTA V and are very likely to return in some form in GTA 6. Everything in this section is a prediction based on GTA V's template. Nobody has confirmed a single GTA 6 cheat. GTA 6 guide Grand Theft Auto VI: Everything We KnowRelease date, map, characters, gameplay and more, updated regularly. For example, GTA 6 will have two playable protagonists. Jason and Lucia open the door to character-specific cheats or something tied to swapping between them. The Leonida setting is Florida, which means hurricanes and tropical storms are a natural fit for weather cheats in Vice City in a way they never were in Los Santos, and with the water and physics work Rockstar has been showing off, boat and underwater cheats could be more interesting than the usual "spawn a jetski" fare.

title: GTA 6 Scam Warning Signs and What to Do if You Get Caught
body: If someone offers you Grand Theft Auto VI for $40, it is a scam. If a website says you can play GTA 6 early, it is a scam. If an email asks for your PlayStation password to confirm your pre-order, it is a scam. With months to go before launch, the GTA 6 scams are going to get worse because the demand is enormous and scammers will, well, scam. This guide is here to make sure you do not lose your money, identity, or anything else related to the next Grand Theft Auto. If you've already found yourself caught by one of these, skip to the last section. There are things you can (or, rather, must) do right now to minimize the potential fallout. First things first, the best thing to do is to buy only from official console stores or a big-name retailer you have heard of and could walk into. The overwhelming majority of GTA 6 scams work by getting you to buy somewhere unfamiliar, so simply refusing to do that eliminates almost all of your risk. Just one important caveat when you are buying. The physical copy is a case with a download code, not a disc. If a site is advertising a physical GTA 6 disc, that alone tells you it is either a scam or does not know what it is selling. Rockstar is not making a disc. Anyone promising you one is lying. GTA 6 Scam Red Flags Warning signs that a GTA 6 offer, site, or message is fraudulent, and why each one matters. No real company, ever, under any circumstances, will ask for your account password by email. If you get an email asking you to log in to "confirm" or "verify" your GTA 6 pre-order, it is phishing, and the goal is to steal your entire gaming account. Phishing usually starts with a scammer sending an email that looks exactly like a real PlayStation or Xbox message, complete with the right logos and formatting. These are usually linked to a fake login page that is a pixel-perfect copy of the real one. You type your email and password, and they now have your account, including any payment methods saved to it. However, this isn't the only scam. There are several other forms of GTA 6 scams floating around. Giveaways, for example, are also very common. However, not every way to get GTA 6 free is a scam. We are running our own GTA 6 giveaway, and legitimate retailers and creators run them too. The difference is in the mechanics. A real giveaway never needs your password, never asks for your payment details, and never asks you to complete a survey or download anything to claim. If a "giveaway" wants any of those, run. title: GTA 6 Hacker Moved to Prison for Retrial Two Years After Indefinite Sentence to Secure Hospital
body: GTA 6 Hacker Moved to Prison for Retrial Two Years After Indefinite Sentence to Secure Hospital Busted. The hacker behind the massive GTA 6 leak in September 2022 has been released from indefinite imprisonment at a secure hospital and is now awaiting a retrial. Arion Kurtaj, a member of the notorious worldwide hacking group named Lapsus$, is waiting for his day in court once again after he was deemed unfit to stand trial in December 2023 due to a medical diagnosis of “acute autism.” After a jury determined that he had committed the hacks, Kurtaj was sentenced to imprisonment at a secure hospital, either for life or until he was deemed medically fit to stand trial again. That time has finally come, as BBC correspondent Joe Tidy confirmed on Bluesky that Kurtaj is now awaiting a retrial in “normal prison,” set to take place in November 2026 — the same month GTA 6 is expected to release. Thus far, nothing else is known about the case, nor how Kurtaj is being retried despite committing the hacking during a period of time when his mental state was so severe that it required hospitalization. Tidy stated on LinkedIn that he’s attempting to glean more information from authorities, but says police are being extremely tight-lipped about the situation to the point that it’s “like getting blood out of a stone.” Kurtaj’s 2022 hack was unprecedented, leaking 90 clips from GTA 6, along with its source code, on a fan forum. What’s more, he was out on bail after hacking NVIDIA and in police protection at a Travelodge when he infiltrated Rockstar’s systems, using only an Amazon Firestick, the TV at his hotel, and a cell phone to pull off the cyberattack. Officials said Kurtaj had expressed violent behavior while in police custody, including "dozens of reports of injury or property damage," with the BBC reporting a mental health assessment that claimed he "continued to express the intent to return to cybercrime as soon as possible. He is highly motivated." GTA 6 releases on November 19, 2026 for PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series X|S. You can find more information about the decade’s most-anticipated title on our hub. Virginia (she/her) is IGN’s News Editor. With ten years of experience reporting on games and entertainment, she’s got a storied background in the fighting game community, influencer news, and viral online trends. Find her on Twitter at @TheeMissGlaze.

title: GTA 6 May Have Already Generated Up to $260 Million in Pre-Orders, Could Surpass $5 Billion at Launch
body:
Having trouble viewing the text? You can always read the original article here:  GTA 6 May Have Already Generated Up to $260 Million in Pre-Orders, Could Surpass $5 Billion at Launch  
  Grand Theft Auto 6  is expected to change the video game industry when it launches on November 19. Even though  the physical editions will not include a game disc , Rockstar Games’ new title is already breaking sales records through pre-orders for PS5 and Xbox Series X|S. 
 Since pre-orders opened on June 25, there have been reports claiming that the game has already earned $1 billion. 
 However, analysis firm Newzoo says those claims are unrealistic because not enough time has passed to reach that amount. 
     
         RELATED 
         
             
                 
                     
                 
             
             
                 
                     
                        GTA 6 Will Require Age Verification in Australia                     
                 
                 
                    GTA 6 is still coming this year to PS5 and Xbox Series X|S, on November 19.                 
             
         
     
    
 GTA 6 Could Make More Than $5 Billion During Launch Week 
   
 According to a report from  Newzoo , GTA 6 has earned around $260 million from physical and digital pre-orders so far. This includes both the Standard Edition and the Ultimate Edition on PS5 and Xbox Series X|S. 
 While the number is very high for only three weeks of sales, Newzoo says it is far more believable than the reports claiming the game has already made $1 billion. 
 The company also believes GTA 6 could earn more than $5 billion during its launch week in November, with total revenue possibly reaching $5.2 billion. With 21 weeks remaining before release, Newzoo considers this target possible for Rockstar Games and Take-Two Interactive. 
 “What the data actually shows is $180 million in digital spend across the US and the five largest European markets in the final week of June, translating to a global opening week of roughly $260 million, with most of the ramp still ahead,” said Ronan Patrick, analyst at Newzoo. 
 The report estimates that GTA 6 could make around $4 billion during its launch week alone. When combined with the money already earned from pre-orders, total revenue could reach $5 billion. 
 The reports adds, “Run that $260 million figure through the plausible band of pre-order curves, and GTA VI is on track to book between $3.25 billion and $5.

title: GTA 6 Pre-Orders Make $260 Million, Launch Week Set For $5 Billion Says Report
body:
GTA 6 has reportedly sold around a quarter of a billion in pre-orders alone so far according to a new report. It is also estimated Rockstar’s new title is on track for upwards of making $5 billion during its release week. 



 Reputable games industry analysis firm  Newzoo  (trusted by game publishers such as Take-Two Interactive has published a report into GTA VI’s pre-order numbers and used their data for launch sales projections. 



 GTA 6 Pre-Order Figures 



 According to the company, their data tells them that  Grand Theft Auto 6  made around $180 million so far in digital pre-orders during its first week of going live. That number is only United States, United Kingdom, France, Italy, Germany, and Spain pre-orders made on the XBOX and PlayStation stores in the last week of June. The  disc-less boxes  are not accounted for here. That makes it the biggest pre-order opening of all time in Newzoo’s dataset. 



 Using that data and GTA V’s global lifetime console player distribution, Newzoo work out that Grand Theft Auto VI has made around $260 million in its first week of pre-orders. That’s more than GTA V’s budget. Newzoo say approximately 69% of GTA V’s lifetime console players are in the 6 countries mentioned above. 



 GTA 6 Opening Week Sales Figures 



   



 Another figure Newzoo have come up with using their data is how much money it will make at the end of the game’s launch week in November. Based on the trajectory of pre-orders, GTA VI is on target to earn between $3.3 billion and $5.2 billion worldwide by November 26th. 



 The firm state it is looking at $4.5 billion by opening week with 51 million copies sold. The average price for those copies is set at $88 as some will buy the $80 standard edition whereas others will grab the $100 Ultimate Edition. 



 Have you pre-ordered the game? Let us know down in the comment section below. 



 You can pre-order your  PS5  or  XBOX  copy with  this link . Using the link to pre-order the game will help support this website as we’ll earn a small commission. Thank you! 



 For more news on GTA VI, stay tuned to  RockstarINTEL  and  sign up  to our newsletter for a weekly round-up of all things Rockstar Games delivered straight to your inbox.

title: GTA 6 Hacker Moved To Prison Ahead Of Criminal Trial
body:
The hacker behind the gigantic  GTA 6 gameplay  leak has been removed from a secure hospital and transferred to a standard prison following his sentencing late 2023. 



 Arion Kurtaj was  found guilty  of numerous charges such as hacking and fraud after a spree of cybercrime. It ended with him accessing Rockstar’s internal communications system and posting 90 videos of GTA VI online years before its release. GTA V’s source code was also published online. Due to his autism and a threat to reoffend, Kurtaj  was placed  in a hospital indefinitely for life. However, that has now changed. 



 We first got wind of this occurrence back in March. We  reported  on how Kurtaj had smuggled a phone into the prison and was gloating on Snapchat about how “amazing” his time in the hospital was. However, there was never an official update from the UK’s legal system. 



 Justice Ahead 



   



 As spotted by  @FriendlyAaron1 , BBC reporter  Joe Tidy , Kurtaj has indeed been shipped off to a standard prison where he will remain at least until a retrial takes place. The journalist explains that reporting restrictions by the courts kept coverage like this from being shared by the media. As of July 14th, the High Court judge lifted said reporting restrictions meaning Tidy could confirm Kurtaj was moved into a prison cell. 



 That’s not the end of Kurtaj’s story. As per Tidy, the hacker will be facing a traditional criminal trial in November this year. This is because he has been deemed medically fit by doctors to stand trial again. We’ll have to wait and see how this plays out. Based on how it went last time, it does not seem like a hopeful conclusion for the young hacker. 



 To keep up to date with every GTA 6 news update, make sure to check back to  RockstarINTEL  and  sign up  to our newsletter for a weekly round-up of all things Rockstar Games.

title: Grand Theft Auto VI First Week Preorders Have Reportedly Generated $260 Million In Global Digital Spending
body:
Analytics firm Newzoo has stated that Rockstar Games’ upcoming   Grand Theft Auto VI  ‘s preorder campaign is currently is tracking as the strongest on record so far, and is looking to sell in the region of 37-51 million copies worldwide in its first week alone. 
 The company stated that about $180 million has been spent on digital preorders for Grand Theft Auto VI in the US and the five largest markets in Europe during the last week of June 2026. It further added that, based on player distribution of Grand Theft Auto V as a guide, total global spending was about $260 million during the first week that preorders went live. 
 “ That kicks off the strongest preorder campaign ever recored, ” said Newzoo. It also predicted first week sales for Rockstar Games’ upcoming crime romp using the same data, noting that hit titles typically follow three sales curves — the brand new IP, the “sequel with performance uncertainty,” and the “proven sequel.” It believes that GTA VI will follow the model of the proven sequence — where more preorders are booked early — and estimates that first-week preorders sales will make up 5.8% of total sales by the end of GTA VI’s first week. 
 
 Contrary to social media reports, GTA 6 has not done a billion dollars in pre-orders 21 weeks out. This is absurd. Given how pre-order curves look, nothing ever has and nothing ever will in the near future. What the data actually shows is $180 million in digital pre-order spend across the US and the five largest European markets in the final week of June, translating to a global opening week of roughly $260 million, with most of the ramp still ahead. 
 Run that figure through the plausible band of preorder curves, and GTA VI is on track to book between $3.25 billion and $5.2 billion in week-one launch revenue. Even at the most conservative reading, namely that GTA 6 front-loads harder than any major title in our dataset, it lands at a tremendous number by any historical standard. 
 
 Grand Theft Auto VI is slated for release on PS5 and Xbox Series X/S on November 19, 2026. Rockstar stirred up a lot of controversy recently after confirming that the  game will only be available digitally at launch ; even the boxed copy of GTA VI will feature a download code, not an actual disc. Worryingly, it has been claimed that a disc version of the game is not planned even ‘ months after launch .’ 
  [Source –  Newzoo  via  VGC ]  
 The post  Grand Theft Auto VI First Week Preorders Have Reportedly Generated $2

title: Rockstar Games accused of weaponizing bonuses by GTA 6 developers
body: Rockstar Games is once again facing criticism from its own workforce after members of the Rockstar Game Workers Union accused the studio of ignoring pay inequity, relying on crunch, and weaponizing bonuses. The allegations were made during an interview with Game Developer, where three union members claimed Rockstar is failing to properly support staff despite being behind some of the biggest games in the industry, like GTA and Red Dead Redemption. The interview comes after a turbulent period for Rockstar. In recent months, there have been rumors that Rockstar employees could strike before GTA 6 launches. Those tensions followed layoffs in late October, when more than 30 staff members were dismissed, prompting the Independent Workers’ Union of Great Britain to accuse the company of “union-busting.” The dispute ultimately led to the formation of the Rockstar Game Workers Union. Now, they’re calling the company out once more. Rockstar employees criticize pay, bonuses, and crunch One of the biggest complaints raised centered on Rockstar’s bonus system, which employees claim is “disappointing” and has a major impact on their annual income. “When the bonus is particularly good, it can be a windfall, but often the bonus is disappointing, and one can end up being paid considerably less than expected for the year. The reasoning given for this is often nebulous, inconsistent between departments, even inconsistent between team members within the same department, and sometimes hinges on completely subjective or retroactive criticisms.” Rather than criticizing bonuses themselves, the employees argued that the issue is how they’re determined. According to the union members, staff are often left without a clear explanation for why payouts differ: “The main problem is that [bonuses and progression are] all completely discretionary for the company, and they are under no obligation to show their working.” The workers also claimed that some employees remain “woefully underpaid for their effort and the incredible profits gained,” arguing that career progression and compensation are ultimately controlled by management without transparent criteria. Related What’s more, one employee highlighted their approach to overtime, claiming: “It seems the company thinks that offering specific and limited compensation as an incentive for overtime means it no longer qualifies as crunch.” They also pointed to employment contracts, saying: “Crunch is prevalent enough that the company built in

title: Rockstar Games could strike before GTA 6 launches over unionization
body: Rockstar Games employees are attempting to unionize before the launch of Grand Theft Auto 6 after a number of devs working on the game were fired. In late October, Rockstar Games fired over 30 members of staff across studios in the United Kingdom and Canada. The Grand Theft Auto developers were accused of ‘union-busting’ over the firings by The Independent Workers’ Union of Great Britain, but said they’d come as a result of “gross misconduct” by the former employees. The firings led to protests outside of Rockstar North’s studio in Edinburgh, Scotland, as well as at Take-Two’s offices in London. Then-UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer called the firings “a deeply concerning” situation, and pledged to investigate. Since then, the fired developers have launched legal action against Rockstar, as well as starting a Rockstar Game Workers Union. Rockstar devs seek formal recognition of union Now, according to The Guardian, they are seeking official recognition as a union well before Grand Theft Auto 6 launches on November 19. Jordan Garland, who was let go back in October after 11 years at Rockstar, said they were looking to “ensure this sort of thing never happens again, not just at Rockstar but anywhere in the industry.” Shanti Easton-Steel, production coordinator at Rockstar North, added that the dismissals were “painful” but they believe they are now in a “strong enough position” to “pursue formal recognition.” According to The Guardian’s report, strike action has been mooted if Rockstar does not move to voluntarily recognize the union. Related The IWGB said that the filing has happened as of June 30. “Today, we’ve filed for recognition with Rockstar Games, asking Rockstar to meet with their workers to agree a deal for good working conditions, fairness and a say at work. We await Rockstar’s response.” Rockstar has since responded to the union’s request, with a spokesperson saying: “We are proud that, as a result, our employee retention is well above the industry standard. “We have received a request from a union seeking to discuss voluntary recognition. We value an open and constructive dialogue with all stakeholders and will arrange to meet.” They announced that the highly anticipated game is still on track, and will launch at $80 for the standard edition, with a deluxe edition costing $100. There has also been backlash over Rockstar’s decision to not provide discs in physical copies, instead sending players a digital download code.
